pending.body: `src/contexts/workflow_composition/prompt_review.rs:328-339` and `src/contexts/project_run_record/service.rs:426-435` now require `validate_current_canonical_prompt_shape`, which rejects canonical v1 prompts that legitimately omit `## Nearby work`. That bypasses the compatibility path intentionally added in `src/contexts/project_run_record/task_prompt_contract.rs:298-306`, so prompt review and `project create-from-bead --prompt-file ...` can no longer accept previously generated canonical task prompts unless the caller invents a Nearby work section it was never given. Keep the stricter validator for freshly generated prompts, but preserve legacy-compatible validation when the source prompt predates this section or version-bump the contract before making the new heading mandatory everywhere.

`src/cli/project.rs:2457-2460` drops every non-`blocks` relation from `Nearby work`, so parent/child edges never appear in `direct_dependencies` or `direct_dependents`. Because bead creation still accepts any open/in-progress bead type (`src/cli/project.rs:1396-1423`), epic beads lose all child-bead scope summaries in `Direct dependents`, and child beads lose their parent epic in `Direct dependencies`, even though the bead spec says these subsections should summarize the focal bead's direct dependency/dependent IDs. If you want to avoid duplicate parent-epic noise for leaf beads, that needs a narrower rule than excluding the entire `parent-child` relation class.

status_summary: running: final review restart round 4 -> Plan and Implement
